title: Request for ability to enter sub Items on a form
excerpt: Hello! We would love to use Monday for our Expense Reimbursements and get rid of the spreadsheets, but   right now there doesn’t appear to be a way to create a new item via form and also break out sub-items. The need is …

posts.author: lizpfahler (Elizabeth Pfahler)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello! We would love to use Monday for our Expense Reimbursements and get rid of the spreadsheets, but right now there doesn’t appear to be a way to create a new item via form and also break out sub-items. The need is to be able to list all the individual expenses and have only totals on the main item.

Sub items inlude expense type, amount etc. Thank for you consideration!

image1076×111 3.72 KB

There’s a conversation on this topic, but not a feedback entry so I thought I’d add one

posts.author: Carson (Carson Butcher)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi @lizpfahler I think this is a great suggestion! I can think of many applications where this would be beneficial and streamline the data input process.

posts.author: psperry (Accidental Selfie )
posts.cookedHtml: Adding this functionality would be huge for me.

posts.author: chrisclick (Chris)
posts.cookedHtml:
Yes, this is much needed!

And also the ability to mirror subitems.

posts.author: tommy_H (Tom)
posts.cookedHtml:
Yep still waiting lol

On another note… Good to see pro users don’t get conditional questions! Boo! Any update when Pro users get to use this? Was told it would be for Pro and Enterprise…

posts.author: fureverafter (Michelle)
posts.cookedHtml: Can’t believe this is being so grossly overlooked by the Monday team. It’s a huge sore point for me and I’m reconsidering where to even keep using Monday.com at all. Other little things I can forgive, but this is a massive time killer for our business, and having to pay for multiple other programs to find a work-around that only partially even works is a total waste of time and money. Very disappointing.

posts.author: psperry (Accidental Selfie )
posts.cookedHtml: I’ve been able to use automations as a work around for now. Would be nice to get some sort of sign that Monday is committed to sub items. Wondering if I’m wasting my time. Many ways to get from here to there…

posts.author: BizFocusedHarry (Harry)
posts.cookedHtml: Monday…please…We need this…

posts.author: BizFocusedHarry (Harry)
posts.cookedHtml: @TRB-monday.com here it is again!

posts.author: TRB-monday.com (Talia)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @lizpfahler, @Carson, @psperry, @chrisclick, @tommy_H, @fureverafter, and @BizFocusedHarry! Can you all please share some use cases with me? What would be most helpful would be a) a sentence or a couple of sentences about the use case OR b) a loom video sent to me in a DM describing the use case.

This will help us prioritize this feature moving forward!

posts.author: psperry (Accidental Selfie )
posts.cookedHtml:
My use case is pretty much like several I have heard. My workflow includes items that typically have around 20 subitems that we use pretty much like a checklist. Subitems are better for us than a simple list because the subitems can be assigned and tracked individually. I had originally envisioned adding these subitems with a form since that is how the item is created in the first place. The work-around I am currently using is a automation that adds the subitems after the item is created. I think for me this is actually preferable because automating this is easier on the user and I am also able to apply certain lists to their corresponding item type, another task that would pushed to the user. Currently my biggest pain point in this with regard is maintaining my subitem lists. This is painful to do in the automations themselves because we don’t have a good editor. In the best of all possible worlds, I would like to maintain my “lists” in subitem templates and then apply them using an automation. This would make the automations easier to manage but I don’t believe that is currently possible.

Sorry, this was more than a couple sentences.

posts.author: nikkige (Nikki GE)
posts.cookedHtml: We were trying recently to create a kind of timesheet to track project tasks for some contract staff, and it would have been good to be able to create a form where the item is the date, day of week and high level details, and the subitems are the tasks with the time spent. Ideally this would be submitted each week via a form.

posts.author: fureverafter (Michelle)
posts.cookedHtml:
We run a pet crematorium.
For us, the parent item is the used to show the main details of who the job is for - the pet, their owner, contact info, etc.
The tasks for this pet are then listed as subitems.
When a booking form is entered, we’d like to be able to create the item WITH subitems as per those the requests selected on the booking form.
Currently, unless we pay for yet ANOTHER program (integromat), as spend ages creating a work around and yet another thing that can “break” in the chain, we have no way of being able to get these subitems created per the booking and have to spend time manually entering each one.
Major hassle. And have already spent several hours trying to get integromat to do it right with unreliable results.

posts.author: kmcdonald (Kirsten McDonald)
posts.cookedHtml: We use items as accounts, then our subitems show what type of cash flow is going in or out. If we could have subitems on forms we could specify what type of flow each subitem was and from there we could have dependent questions on forms based on flow type, then when the form was submitted automations and formulas could take over to delineate actual need for each cash flow.

posts.author: MTCmonz (Marie Thelma Cardozo)
posts.cookedHtml:
any update on this feature?
I don’t see it on their product plan Subitems Product Plan

Currently, I’m using Superform to connect to a board item. Would be better if it was just nested under as a sub-item form submission

posts.author: shawn.yarnold (Shawn Yarnold)
posts.cookedHtml:
I absolutely need this functionality!! Great suggestion. I was hoping I could do this now, but unfortunately it’s not available.
My use case is for New User/Onboarding - Form is submitted for each new hire or exiting employee - sub-items are used for security, hardware and software needs.

posts.author: lizpfahler (Elizabeth Pfahler)
posts.cookedHtml: Sorry for the delay, I missed this! My use case is that we wanted to use it for Expense reimbursement. Where it would be a single line item for the entire amount requested, but sub items for the individual expenses. Finance wants to review the individual expenses, but want’s to approve and process the sum of the expense request.

posts.author: Michael-PT (Michael Albers)
posts.cookedHtml:
My use case would be that I add vehicle details as a subitem to a contact.
Then, based on the vehicle details, we create a proposal (that probably will go to another board) and after acceptance a project item on a project board will be created.

Currently putting the vehicle details in a seperate board, but as a new user I find it a bit of a hassle to get things linked the way I would like. Still got a lot to learn to get all boards linked correctly.

posts.author: miguelt (Miguel Thomas)
posts.cookedHtml: Not sure if this is still being looked at, but our use case is for a simple Purchase Request. Currently if someone is requesting multiple items, they have to submit multiple copies of the form. It would be much nicer to be able to populate subitems, this saves purchasing and submitter great deals of time and keeps boards cleaner and more organized.

posts.author: JustineBrice (Justine Brice)
posts.cookedHtml: This would really help us. Our use case is that our item is our IT “problem” and we have to submit multiple request forms for changes to fix that problem. Each request form is currently tracked as a subitem. If we could create a form to populate subitems, it would really help our workflow.

posts.author: jmee (Joshua Mee)
posts.cookedHtml: This feature is very important for us. We need to be able to send a form in which an order can be filled having specific devices with distinct quantities and customizations listed as subitems.

posts.author: sabrescia (Sarah Brescia)
posts.cookedHtml: Please add this functionality!!! I want to set up a form for employee reviews with several sub-items under a header, but no way to do this currently.

posts.author: lindsey (Lindsey Dowthwaite)
posts.cookedHtml: This is exactly the reason we need this feature. Perfect for expense claims. And I am sure lots of other scenarios

posts.author: ilariatottolo2 (ilaria)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi! Is there anyone that knows an app that allows items and subitems (columns and sub-columns) on the form?
This would be great for an eCommerce use type: where the item corresponds to the order and the subitems are the ordered articles.

posts.author: Tammy37 (Tammy Ball)
posts.cookedHtml: Hello I am also very interested in this feature. I have voted and following.

posts.author: lindsaydreece (Lindsay Reece)
posts.cookedHtml: Is there any progress with this feature request? Multiple people have been requesting it for the past 2 years. We are a subcontractor and looking for a way for our infield crews to be able to dispatch tasks and crews as subitems for each install and track completion. we don’t want them to create a new main item on the board because it will create duplications. Having them create it in the field as a form and it populating to the subitem. This would keep all install tasks and history under the main install item.

posts.author: RezaMPG (Reza Jahangir Tafrechi)
posts.cookedHtml: come-on MONDAY gives us a sign please. we experience the same strugle regarding forms and subitems

posts.author: guy.gavish (Guygav)
posts.cookedHtml: You can do it with SuperForm app

posts.author: Julietteb (Juliette )
posts.cookedHtml: Hi Everyone, happy to share that this feature is coming soon! Our team is working on adding the ability to enter subitems on forms. Before this feature is released, our team is working on releasing the new form builder. Will update when I have more information about the timeline!

posts.author: Todd (Todd Rosenblatt)
posts.cookedHtml: Is this solution still not available? Is there an ETA of when it will be rolled out?

posts.author: Fantasy-Media-Team (Fantasy Media Team)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @Todd, @RezaMPG, @lindsaydreece, @Tammy37, @ilariatottolo2, @lindsey, @sabrescia, @jmee, @JustineBrice

We developed an app called Google Forms Integration Plus that allows getting responses from Google Forms directly monday.com.
Google Forms is free to use and has advanced capabilities that are missing in monday’s forms, and our app offers more flexible and robust logic. It basically solves all the gaps in monday’s forms.

You can easily create subitems based on responses from forms. Here are 2 examples.

Creating on a specific item →

Screenshot 2024-10-02 at 16.15.181820×280 16.4 KB

Creating on a dynamic item (based on the response in the form) →

Screenshot 2024-10-02 at 16.15.582084×348 32 KB

You can also update an existing subitem →

Screenshot 2024-10-02 at 16.18.012228×332 34.8 KB

You can find it in the monday.com marketplace in the platform or here - Google Forms Integration Plus - monday.com App Marketplace

Hope that helps!

posts.author: Cyrino (JP Nonnet)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @lizpfahler, this is a great suggestion!

We are looking for the same functionality for marketing campaign entries. Currently we have been evaluating Superform from the app marketplace as it allows you to create subitems directly from the form.

Question for you Monday/Monday community folks if this feature is in the pipeline for this year, is it worth investment in Superform?

posts.author: Fantasy-Media-Team (Fantasy Media Team)
posts.cookedHtml: Hey @Cyrino - see my post above. You can simply achieve that with Google Forms which is a super powerful and free tool and our integration to monday.com

posts.author: Cyrino (JP Nonnet)
posts.cookedHtml:
Thanks @Fantasy-Media-Team , we will definitely look into and evaluate your solution.

Best, JP

posts.author: ManicMondayUser (John Mc)
posts.cookedHtml: Still no sign of this being available, could we have an update please and an ETA for sub item entry via form

posts.author: Fantasy-Media-Team (Fantasy Media Team)
posts.cookedHtml: Hey @ManicMondayUser - see my post above. You can simply achieve that with Google Forms which is a super powerful and free tool and our integration to monday.com

posts.author: Lblando (Luke Blando)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi, any update on this feature? Here is our current workflow, we use this form on our contact us page of our website which once submitted, depending on type of inquiry, links to the specific department, ie Sales, Marketing, or HR. The person assigned then creates a subitem for a follow up and so on. We want to make this simpler for our sales team who should be able to open the parent submission view and then have subitem next to it to document the follow up properly.

Thanks!

posts.author: Fantasy-Media-Team (Fantasy Media Team)
posts.cookedHtml:

Google Forms is free to use and has advanced capabilities that are missing in monday’s forms, and our app offers more flexible and robust logic. It basically solves all the gaps in monday’s forms.

You can easily create subitems based on responses from forms. Here are 2 examples.

posts.author: Cyrino (JP Nonnet)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @Julietteb,

This feature is crucial for our enterprise customers. Currently, we’re using Superform, but we’ve learned from our experience that heavily regulated enterprises prefer not to work with third-party developers due to security concerns.

When can we expect Monday.com to implement the ability to create subitems or multiple subitems directly from the native Monday form, similar to the functionality offered by Superform?

Thank you for your attention to this matter!

posts.author: amybronstein (Amy Bronstein)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi everyone! Amy here from the monday.com team I’m VERY excited to share that the ability to add subitems via forms is now live for Pro and ENT plans!

With the new subitems question type, you can now collect more detailed and structured information directly through your forms!

It allows you to:
Add subitem questions for deeper data collection
Customize column titles to match your needs
Drag and drop to reorder for easier form building
Choose to show or hide specific subquestions

Thank you for all your feedback, it’s been crucial in bringing this feature to life!
